text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill. Good time management begins with breaking large projects into smaller tasks and setting mini-deadlines for each. Prioritization helps you focus on what’s most important first, rather than simply doing the easiest thing. Scope management is equally vital: if a project grows too big, learn to say no or adjust your plan. Finally, open communication with your team or clients can prevent last-minute surprises. If you anticipate a delay, inform others early, so solutions can be found together. With these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less stress.
